scientific_integrity.detail: Explicit prohibition on fabricating trial status, approval status, company activity, and asset metadata

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

reliability.note: Step 2 live-verification assumption; no explicit fallback for offline environments

performance_context.note: 294 lines; eight reference modules well organized

agent_usability.note: Same live-verification assumption issue as other evidence skills

agent_specific.note: Development maturity + strategic openness dual framework is a valuable differentiator; crowding vs maturity separation is strong

inputs.label: TIGIT target evidence landscape in solid tumors

assertions.text: Disease relevance, druggability, preclinical evidence, and clinical evidence separated
Competition and crowding map includes same-target and substitute approaches
Development maturity and strategic openness assessed separately
No fabricated trial identifiers, approval status, or company activity
Primary recommended interpretation given with limiting-factor identification

inputs.label: IL-17 pathway competition and strategic whitespace in psoriasis

assertions.text: Target relevance separated from druggability
Crowded field not automatically labeled as closed without checking differentiation logic
Strategic openness section identifies remaining whitespace if any
Preclinical activity not presented as proof of patient benefit
Verification caveat applied when asset/trial details are mentioned

inputs.label: KRAS G12D vs SHP2 target comparison in pancreatic cancer

assertions.text: Two targets compared using same evidence-layer framework
Tractability comparison explicit for both targets
Evidence chain gaps explicitly identified for each target
No fabricated relative efficacy or superiority claims without clinical evidence
Self-critical review identifies most assumption-dependent comparison point

inputs.label: Early-stage target with only preclinical evidence and no clinical data

assertions.text: Clinical evidence section correctly marked as absent with explicit statement
Preclinical activity not implied as proof of clinical promise
Development maturity correctly assigned as early-stage
Sparse competition not presented as automatic strategic attractiveness
Verification gap flagged as limiting factor in primary interpretation

inputs.label: Ferroptosis-related interventions in HCC — full landscape with multiple modalities

assertions.text: Multiple modalities assessed with separate tractability and competition analysis
Biology relevance and therapeutic tractability not conflated for any modality
Mixed evidence (thin on some paths, stronger on others) represented accurately
Primary interpretation states limiting factor explicitly
No fabricated clinical trial activity for ferroptosis HCC assets

key_strengths: Development maturity + strategic openness dual framework prevents the common error of conflating crowded space with closed opportunity
Eight-layer evidence separation (disease relevance, mechanistic rationale, druggability, preclinical, translational bridge, clinical, competition, maturity) is uniquely comprehensive
Asset and trial verification requirement with explicit caveat obligation prevents fabricated pipeline claims
Negative trigger guidance ('do not present a tractable target as disease-relevant unless evidence is explicit') is a rare and valuable guard against reverse-logic errors

recommendations.title: Step 2 live verification assumption creates inconsistency in offline environments
recommendations.problem: Step 2 requires verifying trial status, approvals, and asset data from live sources, but in offline execution this defaults to training memory which may produce outdated or incorrect competitive landscape claims.
recommendations.root_cause: No fallback behavior defined for environments without live database access.
recommendations.fix: Add explicit fallback: 'When live verification is unavailable, all trial status, approval status, and competitive activity claims must be labeled [UNVERIFIED — based on training knowledge, verify with ClinicalTrials.gov and DrugBank before use]'.

recommendations.title: No guidance on how to handle proprietary or undisclosed asset information
recommendations.problem: The skill handles public asset information well but does not specify how to respond when users provide proprietary pipeline information they want incorporated into the landscape.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section focuses on public evidence sources and does not address user-provided proprietary context.
recommendations.fix: Add a note: 'If the user provides proprietary or confidential asset information, incorporate it into the landscape analysis and label it [User-provided — not independently verifiable]'.
